updates.trigger: after each successfully committed signed heartbeat
updates.trust: The server may offer only the exact official GitHub repository, stable version, tag, full commit, asset name, and SHA-256. The plugin independently resolves the official GitHub tag and published release, downloads through a bounded GitHub redirect chain, verifies SHA-256, and validates/extracts the archive without links or traversal.
updates.activation: an immutable versions/<version> directory plus one atomic active-release.json switch through the stable current-user launcher
updates.failurePolicy: an update failure never rolls back or invalidates an already committed sync or heartbeat; the current and previous releases remain available

releaseArtifact.releaseContract: require package @the-artificial-games/tag-plugin; reject -local; require tag v<package-version>; require package, runtime constant, and manifest versions to match; require archive tag-plugin-<package-version>.tgz

localReads.surface: Antigravity CLI statusLine JSON through TAG's consented wrapper
localReads.purpose: prospective usage, plan, and quota-window observations; no historical backfill
local activity diagnostics only; never token accounting or an uploaded usage event

localWrites: pending-device-secrets.json (only while pairing or replacing a device)
.<known-runtime-json>.<pid>.<12-hex>.tmp (atomic write only; stale exact files are reclaimed under the overlap lock only when the committed counterpart remains a regular file)
antigravity-statusline-state.json (only after explicit Antigravity wrapper installation; saves prior statusLine value for restoration)
<Antigravity home>/tag-plugin-statusline.jsonl (sanitized prospective capture only after explicit Antigravity wrapper installation)
connector.lock (ephemeral owner-token lease during state-changing commands)
sync-pages/<batch-id>/*.json (temporary content-free future pages during catch-up)

localState.pendingDeviceSecrets: separate unpaired Ed25519 key until exchange succeeds; does not overwrite an active device during replacement
localState.pairingRecovery: short-lived code, public key, endpoint, device label, connector version, approved fallbacks, private key, and fixed exchange request are stored only in the restricted pending-secret journal until config-first/state-last commit succeeds
localState.syncRecovery: signed heartbeat hydration reads the current Codex account snapshot before every eligible plan; Codex snapshot generations follow, with generationId equal to SHA-256(parentGenerationId plus NUL plus the canonical snapshot digest), only changed daily values tagged daily_delta, and one final lifetime commit marker carrying the generation, parent, full snapshot digest, and delta count; every checkpoint requires exact canonical-digest acknowledgement and the marker additionally requires active-generation proof; stale-parent recovery hydrates the winner, retires only checkpoint work, and preserves durable event pages; the submitted generation, digest, lifetime value, and daily map persist locally only after proof and the final outbox item succeed; then newest-first catch-up pages capped at 5000 hourly aggregate events each; active page embedded in state and future content-free pages stored privately under sync-pages/<batch-id> with an event-count/digest manifest; fixed active request plus disjoint chunks of at most 3 events or 2 checkpoints, paced 550 ms apart; missing or corrupt pages fail closed; collection cursors commit only after the final page; interactive sync processes at most 100 requests; scheduled runs process at most 1000 ingest requests, heartbeat every 50 and once at the end even with backlog
localState.historyImport: all safely discoverable retained journal history from the Unix epoch, independently of the server score-eligibility horizon; local source-ID deduplication; stable provider/source-session/UTC-hour/raw-model/raw-mode/surface aggregation without sharding; disabled, unavailable, truncated, or event-limited providers retain their prior through-watermark; malformed or oversized records keep coverage explicitly partial with a privacy-safe count while a completed scan range may advance so later valid usage is not pinned; later runs normally scan only newly settled hours
localState.aggregateWatermarks: independent Codex, Claude, and Kimi settled-through times staged per complete provider and committed only after the final sync page
localState.unresolvedModels: unknown-model or unknown-mode usage uploads immediately as an atomic raw-only event without a local queue; modelId and serviceMode are both the unknown sentinel, attribution is raw_only, source model tokens never upload, and a one-time stable-ID backfill repairs the retired queue generation
localState.eventIdentity: raw source IDs are account-scoped HMACs over content-independent source identity and deduplicate locally; Codex and Claude logical aggregates retain the released v4 identity domain and Kimi retains v3, binding provider, stable source-session scope, UTC hour, raw source-model token, raw mode token, and coding surface independently of parser/accounting generation and raw-versus-attributed classification; source-ID set, totals, canonical model mapping, and collector provenance are excluded from identity but changed canonical observation fields produce a new payload revision under the same event ID; raw source IDs and identity inputs do not upload as separate fields
localState.planAndResetObservations: a signed heartbeat may contain bounded content-free provider plan observations and actual quota-window reset observations. Codex plan/rate-window values come from official app-server account RPCs; Claude plan comes from claude auth status JSON; Antigravity values come only from the explicitly installed sanitized statusLine wrapper. A scheduled reset is emitted when a previously observed quota-window boundary advances. Within the same future window, a manual reset is emitted only after a locally retained usage percentage falls by at least 30 percentage points; percentages never leave the computer. DeepSeek api_payg is reported only when explicit API evidence exists; a hosted DeepSeek model never creates that entitlement.
localState.operationLock: random owner token, open-handle lease renewed every stale-window quarter, live-PID stale-takeover guard, atomic rename/revalidation, and ownership-checked release
localState.atomicWriteRecovery: atomic rename is the only replacement step; Windows EEXIST or EPERM retries that same primitive five times with bounded delay, then fails closed without unlinking the prior committed JSON and removes only that operation's temp; after the 15-minute lease window, state-changing startup may remove only exact known runtime-JSON temp names under connector home, only while proving ownership of the canonical overlap lock, only when both the temp and its unchanged committed counterpart are regular files and the encoded PID is no longer live; a temp that is the sole recovery copy is preserved; root and direct sync-page scans are non-recursive and bounded
localState.pathPrivacy: raw paths are not persisted; installation-keyed HMAC aliases are persisted locally as cursor keys
localState.cursorReplacementDetection: file metadata, complete-line boundary, and rolling prefix digest over allowlisted usage/model/mode fields only; prompt/tool content is never hashed

network.origin: pairing argument, HTTPS required except explicit localhost
network.eventCommitAcknowledgement: every submitted event requires exactly one matching response that explicitly reports submittedRevisionActive true, or submittedObservationCanonical true for an exact content duplicate under a different logical event; status and aggregate counts never authorize cursor commit and an isolated permanent rejection fails closed
network.rawOnlyCommit: in addition to exact event acknowledgement, each raw-only event requires a matching per-event rawPreserved true response before its local source cursor can commit; score status may be accepted, quarantined, duplicate, or superseded

retry.strategy: exponential backoff with jitter or bounded standards-compliant Retry-After
retry.permanent4xx: no blind retry; eligible invalid batches are bisected and an isolated unacknowledged event or checkpoint blocks without committing its source cursor

scheduler.postInstall: send one immediate allowlisted usage sync and signed heartbeat before the first hourly trigger; that heartbeat also checks the pinned stable update offer

permissions.windowsSecretAcl: before credential creation restrict the connector directory to one inheritable current-user grant; before pairing traffic explicitly harden the pending secret; before pairing commit harden the final secret; before install re-verify the final secret; fail closed on any error

uninstall.antigravityStatusLine: when TAG still owns the configured wrapper, uninstall restores the saved prior statusLine (or removes TAG's entry if none existed). A later user change is preserved; TAG only removes its private capture/state files.

codingAgentPrompt.websiteGeneratedModeDisclosure: before copying the one-message prompt, disclose that the selected coding-agent provider receives and may retain the short-lived pair code under its normal terms
codingAgentPrompt.saferAlternative: enter the pair code directly in a local terminal after agent review
